players.name: Eloy Room
players.bio: Curaçao's rock between the sticks for over a decade, Eloy Room brings 71 caps and veteran composure to his first World Cup at 37. The Netherlands-born keeper has been the undisputed No.1 throughout Curaçao's rise.
players.facts: At 37, Room is one of the oldest goalkeepers at the 2026 World Cup — older than some nations' entire squads.

players.name: Shurandy Sambo
players.bio: Young Sparta Rotterdam right-back Shurandy Sambo is one of Curaçao's fresh faces, earning his place with energetic displays on the flank. Just 24, the Geldrop-born defender arrives at his first major tournament with only 8 caps to his name.
players.facts: Sambo was born in Geldrop — a Dutch town so small it once had more cows than people. Now he's at a World Cup.

players.name: Juriën Gaari
players.bio: A seasoned defensive anchor with 60 caps, Juriën Gaari has been a cornerstone of Curaçao's backline for years. The Kerkrade-born centre-back brings tournament experience and calm authority — even if goals aren't really his department.
players.facts: Gaari has scored just 1 goal in 60 international appearances. At that rate, his next one is due around 2085.

players.name: Roshon van Eijma
players.full_name: Roshon Felix Romar Jordan van Eijma
players.bio: Tilburg-born Roshon van Eijma is a composed and physical centre-back who has quietly become a key figure in Curaçao's defensive setup. His 28 caps include a rare international goal, proving even defenders get their moment.
players.facts: Van Eijma plays for RKC Waalwijk — a club whose name is so tricky, most commentators just call them RKC and move on.

players.name: Sherel Floranus
players.bio: Rotterdam-born Sherel Floranus brings pace and tenacity to Curaçao's right flank from his base at PEC Zwolle. With 28 caps, he's a trusted option in a squad packed with Dutch Eredivisie experience.
players.facts: Floranus plays right-back for Curaçao while club teammate pressures mount in Zwolle's relegation battles — multitasking at its finest.

players.name: Godfried Roemeratoe
players.bio: Godfried Roemeratoe is a creative and combative midfielder who chips in with the occasional goal for both club RKC Waalwijk and Curaçao. With 28 caps and a knack for hard work in midfield, he's a reliable engine in Nos Seleccion's setup.
players.facts: His name is so magnificent that autocorrect gives up entirely — Roemeratoe simply cannot be contained by spellcheck.

players.name: Juninho Bacuna
players.bio: Juninho Bacuna is Curaçao's most prolific midfielder with 15 goals in 49 caps — a remarkable return for a box-to-box dynamo. The younger Bacuna brother loves a long-range effort and has been one of Nos Seleccion's most consistent performers.
players.facts: Juninho shares his first name with Brazilian legend Juninho Pernambucano — and has clearly absorbed some of that free-kick energy.

players.name: Livano Comenencia
players.bio: Teenage talent Livano Comenencia is one of the most exciting young players in Curaçao's squad, representing FC Zürich at just 22. With 2 goals in 20 caps, the midfielder punches above his years and arrives at his first World Cup full of potential.
players.facts: Born in 2004, Comenencia was only 2 years old when the 2006 World Cup happened. Now he's playing in one.

players.name: Jürgen Locadia
players.bio: Jürgen Locadia enjoyed a high-profile spell in the Premier League with Brighton before his career took a well-travelled path. Now at Miami FC alongside keeper Room, the powerful striker brings top-level pedigree to Curaçao's attack at 32.
players.facts: Locadia once played for Brighton in the Premier League — and now lines up with his Curaçao goalkeeper at Miami FC. Small world.

players.name: Leandro Bacuna
players.bio: The elder Bacuna brother and Curaçao's all-time leading scorer with 16 goals in 72 caps — remarkable numbers for a right-back. Leandro is a set-piece specialist and talismanic captain figure who has led his nation to this historic World Cup.
players.facts: Leandro Bacuna is a right-back with 16 international goals. Most actual forwards would settle for that tally.

players.name: Jeremy Antonisse
players.bio: Pacy left winger Jeremy Antonisse brings flair and directness to Curaçao's attack, earning 27 caps at just 24. The Rosmalen-born winger's goal record and energy make him a dangerous weapon on the counterattack for Nos Seleccion.
players.facts: Antonisse plays for Kifisia in Greece — proof that Curaçao's talent pipeline runs through every corner of European football.

players.name: Sontje Hansen
players.bio: Middlesbrough winger Sontje Hansen is one of Curaçao's most exciting young talents, already scoring on the international stage with 1 goal in 6 caps. The 24-year-old brings English Championship physicality and sharp movement to the right flank.
players.facts: Hansen was named after Brazilian legend Sontje — wait, no. He's just got a brilliant name that sounds like a superhero alias.

players.name: Tyrese Noslin
players.bio: Amsterdam-born Tyrese Noslin is a lively right midfielder who has wasted no time making an impact, scoring in just 7 international appearances. The Telstar man brings youthful intensity and a nose for goal from wide areas.
players.facts: Noslin plays for Telstar — a club named after a 1960s satellite. His career, however, is very much in orbit.

players.name: Kenji Gorré
players.bio: Versatile forward Kenji Gorré has plied his trade across Europe, including stints in England, and now lights up Israeli football with Maccabi Haifa. Six goals in 38 caps shows a reliable striker who delivers when it matters for Curaçao.
players.facts: Gorré has played in England, the Netherlands, Belgium, and Israel. His passport deserves its own trophy cabinet.

players.name: Ar'jany Martha
players.bio: Ar'jany Martha is one of Curaçao's most exciting young midfielders, firing in 2 goals from just 9 caps at Rotherham United. The 22-year-old brings drive and ambition — and an apostrophe in his name that demands double-takes.
players.facts: Ar'jany Martha has the most typographically adventurous name at the 2026 World Cup. That apostrophe is doing serious work.

players.name: Jearl Margaritha
players.bio: Jearl Margaritha is a sharp and technically gifted forward who has been consistently productive for both Beveren and Curaçao, netting 5 goals in 22 caps. An intelligent mover in the box, he offers genuine striking threat off the bench or from the start.
players.facts: Margaritha — a name so elegant it belongs on a cocktail menu. His finishing, fortunately, is equally refined.

players.name: Brandley Kuwas
players.bio: Experienced winger Brandley Kuwas has been a familiar face in Dutch football for years, now back at Volendam after a well-travelled career. With 35 caps, the 33-year-old brings leadership, experience, and the odd moment of magic to Curaçao's attack.
players.facts: Kuwas has been capped 35 times for Curaçao — he's been around longer than most of the squad have held driving licences.

players.name: Armando Obispo
players.bio: PSV Eindhoven defender Armando Obispo is one of the most pedigreed names in this Curaçao squad, bringing Champions League club experience to the national team. With just 6 caps so far, this World Cup could be the stage where he truly announces himself.
players.facts: Obispo trains at PSV alongside some of Europe's elite — yet Curaçao is where he calls home. That's loyalty right there.

players.name: Gervane Kastaneer
players.bio: Rotterdam-born Gervane Kastaneer is Curaçao's joint-top scorer with 9 goals from 29 caps — a remarkable record for a forward who has journeyed from the Netherlands to the jungles of Malaysian football with Terengganu. A true goal-poacher.
players.facts: Kastaneer is now playing in Malaysia's Super League — his career has taken him further geographically than almost any World Cup player.

players.name: Joshua Brenet
players.bio: Experienced full-back Joshua Brenet has enjoyed a solid European career including time in the Bundesliga, and brings that quality to Curaçao's right side. Now at Kayserispor, the Kerkrade-born defender offers tactical intelligence and big-game composure.
players.facts: Brenet has played in the Netherlands, Germany, and Turkey. He's basically doing a tour of European football one league at a time.

players.name: Tahith Chong
players.bio: Born in Willemstad, Curaçao, Tahith Chong is the island's most recognisable export — a former Manchester United academy graduate who dazzled at Old Trafford before carving out a career at Sheffield United. Three goals in just 6 caps shows frightening efficiency.
players.kit: Nike Mercurial Superfly VII Elite FG
players.facts: Chong was once one of Man United's most hyped academy prospects. He swapped Old Trafford for Bramall Lane — and Curaçao for glory.

players.name: Kevin Felida
players.bio: Defensive midfielder Kevin Felida is the unsung engine in Curaçao's midfield, doing the dirty work that allows teammates to shine. The Spijkenisse-born Den Bosch man has 19 caps and a goal to his name — proving even destroyers have their moments.
players.facts: Felida's job is to break up play and protect the defence. He's basically football's equivalent of bubble wrap.

players.name: Riechedly Bazoer
players.bio: Once one of Dutch football's most coveted young talents, Riechedly Bazoer had a turbulent career after Ajax before finding stability at Konyaspor. Now reinvented as a centre-back for Curaçao, his 5 caps represent a compelling new chapter at 29.
players.facts: Bazoer was once compared to Patrick Vieira. A tough act to follow — but at least he's turned up to a World Cup.

players.name: Deveron Fonville
players.bio: Deveron Fonville is Curaçao's newest defensive face with just 2 caps to his name, but the young NEC centre-back has earned his spot through consistent club performances. At 23, this World Cup represents an extraordinary debut on the grandest stage.
players.facts: With just 2 caps, Fonville is the least experienced outfield player in this squad. No pressure at a World Cup, kid.

players.name: Tyrick Bodak
players.bio: Almere-born Tyrick Bodak is the squad's backup goalkeeper and one of its youngest members at 24. The Telstar stopper has 4 caps and will be looking to learn from veteran No.1 Eloy Room while keeping himself sharp for any opportunity.
players.facts: Bodak and Room are Curaçao's goalkeepers — one born in Almere, one in Nijmegen. Both representing an island 8,000km away.

players.name: Trevor Doornbusch
players.bio: Haarlem-born Trevor Doornbusch is Curaçao's third goalkeeper, bringing steady VVV-Venlo form to the tournament. His 8 caps suggest he's more than just a squad filler — though at a World Cup, staying sharp on the training pitch is its own art form.
players.facts: Doornbusch literally translates from Dutch as 'thornbush' — a name suggesting he's not someone you want to test from close range.
